nice_have_skills: familiarity with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP)
understanding of database concepts and SQL
education_requirements: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field
key_responsibilities: Participate in the full software development lifecycle
Write clean, efficient, and well-documented code
Collaborate to troubleshoot and resolve software defects
Learn and apply new technologies and methodologies
Assist in the creation of technical documentation
Participate in team meetings and discussions
relevant_data: Intuit serves approximately 100 million customers worldwide and focuses on AI-driven technology for financial challenges.
The company emphasizes a culture of mobility and career growth, supporting both vertical and lateral advancement opportunities.
Intuit is recognized for good compensation, benefits, and strong work-life balance but has some concerns about internal politics and work-life balance declining in certain teams.
Currently hiring for various IT roles, including engineering and data positions, many with remote work options.
